title: auricula-ursi marginata
intro: Auricula-ursi marginata, also known as bearberry or kinnikinnick, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America and Europe. The plant's leaves and berries have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to treat urinary tract infections, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of auricula-ursi marginata include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe irritated tissues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health and urinary tract wellness.
